summary: No-ship: the optimistic-CAS implementation is still racy, so concurrent ticks can both acquire the same card, mint different runIds, and run the same transition twice.

findings.title: `saveCardCAS` is not atomic, allowing double-acquire and double-mint under concurrent ticks
findings.recommendation: Replace the read-then-rename CAS with an actual atomic claim mechanism (for example a lockfile/open-with-O_EXCL, per-card mutex, or compare-and-link style update) and keep the runId mint inside that single critical section. As written, two workers can both observe the same `rev`, both return `ok: true`, and both proceed through `processCard`/`processBatch`, violating the acceptance requirement that runId/runDir be minted once and CAS-safe under concurrent ticks.
